sources_included.source: Errors and Vulnerabilities in AI-Generated Code.pdf

sources_excluded.reason: Quick-depth preview per Step 10 skip decision
Quick-depth preview per Step 10 skip decision

themes.name: Multi-stage extraction pipelines (fallback-based)
themes.description: Most pipelines couple them; this repo extracts ALL metadata once then runs arbitrary LLM prompts on extracted data.

evidence.quote_or_ref: extraction and analysis are separate problems that should be separate systems. Most video processing pipelines couple them
Per-frame ML feature extraction (extr-srv-image-extraction.py): Each Rekognition API call (labels, text, celebrity, moderation) is independently toggleable
the web crawling problem has bifurcated. There are crawlers built for humans (Scrapy, Beautiful Soup) and crawlers built for machines (crawl4ai)
fetch a URL is not one problem — it's a chain of failure modes. CrawlerHost.cachedScrap() in src/api/crawler.ts:1374 lines runs a layered strategy

themes.name: Plugin systems & hook/lifecycle governance
themes.description: Browser pool with warmth tiers and janitor lifecycle — claims 10x memory reduction. Pattern portable beyond browsers.

evidence.quote_or_ref: The 3-tier browser pool (PERMANENT/HOT/COLD) in the Docker server is production infrastructure thinking. PERMANENT browsers stay warm for frequent clients
Hook lifecycle ordering (docs/md_v2/advanced/hooks-auth.md) -- 8 hooks with clear sequence and explicit guidance on which hook for which purpose
The 10 hook types in server/utils/PluginManager.ts (API, AuthProvider, EmailTemplate, Processor, Task, etc.) with priority-based ordering and auto-discovery is a clean extensibility model
Uses `pytubefix` for download + `moviepy` for audio extraction to MP3. This is the exact YouTube content pipeline T27 needs.

themes.name: Testing, coverage, and verification approaches
themes.description: 52 Python files, 1 test file — AWS reference samples routinely skip tests.

evidence.quote_or_ref: No tests. The tests/ directory has 1 file. Don't follow this pattern.
Zero automated tests. No .test.ts, no .spec.ts, no describe(), nothing. firebase-functions-test sits in devDependencies but is never imported

TestServer Pattern -- MEDIUM. server/test/TestServer.ts creates a real Koa HTTP server on an ephemeral port for integration tests. SoNash tests mock httpsCallable -- the Outline approach tests the actual transport
This is a curated list that invested in structural integrity tooling

themes.name: Claude Code as a platform (skills, plugins, commands, CLAUDE.md)
themes.description: 10 hook types (API, AuthProvider, EmailTemplate, Processor, Task, SearchProvider, etc.) with priority ordering, auto-discovery, plugin.json manifests.

evidence.quote_or_ref: The 10 hook types in server/utils/PluginManager.ts (API, AuthProvider, EmailTemplate, Processor, Task, etc.) with priority-based ordering and auto-discovery is a clean extensibility model
It's not just a Python wrapper for Google NotebookLM -- it's a complete agent-native package: Python API + CLI + SKILL.md + skill install command + multi-agent support + CI
skill.py install mechanism solves a real problem: how do you distribute a SKILL.md to the right location? Read from package data, stamp version, write to ~/.claude/skills/
SKILL.md Autonomy Rules (lines 94-128). 22 commands "Run automatically" + 7 "Ask before running," each with a one-line reason

themes.name: Agent orchestration / multi-agent patterns
themes.description: 11 runtime-control headers (x-target-selector, x-timeout, x-cache-tolerance, x-engine, x-return-format) — cleaner than flags when exposing to agents/MCP.

evidence.quote_or_ref: Eleven runtime-control headers (x-target-selector, x-wait-for-selector, x-timeout, x-cache-tolerance, x-no-cache, x-respond-with, x-proxy-url, x-set-cookie, x-with-generated-alt, x-engine, x-return-format)
It's not just a Python wrapper for Google NotebookLM -- it's a complete agent-native package: Python API + CLI + SKILL.md + skill install command + multi-agent support + CI
skill.py install mechanism solves a real problem: how do you distribute a SKILL.md to the right location? Read from package data, stamp version, write to ~/.claude/skills/
Embedded Task() subagent patterns. Literal Task(prompt=..., subagent_type=...) in SKILL.md workflows. Reduces agent cognitive load

themes.name: Memory / knowledge-graph / note-taking systems
themes.description: Browser pool with warmth tiers and janitor lifecycle — claims 10x memory reduction. Pattern portable beyond browsers.

evidence.quote_or_ref: The 3-tier browser pool (PERMANENT/HOT/COLD) in the Docker server is production infrastructure thinking. PERMANENT browsers stay warm for frequent clients
fetch a URL is not one problem — it's a chain of failure modes. CrawlerHost.cachedScrap() in src/api/crawler.ts:1374 lines runs a layered strategy
A new user could set up the Obsidian system in 5 minutes. Our CAS took an entire session to build
A scheduled or on-push link checker would catch rotting references before they mislead.

themes.name: MCP as a first-class integration surface
themes.description: 11 runtime-control headers (x-target-selector, x-timeout, x-cache-tolerance, x-engine, x-return-format) — cleaner than flags when exposing to agents/MCP.

evidence.quote_or_ref: Eleven runtime-control headers (x-target-selector, x-wait-for-selector, x-timeout, x-cache-tolerance, x-no-cache, x-respond-with, x-proxy-url, x-set-cookie, x-with-generated-alt, x-engine, x-return-format)

The command pattern (server/commands/) is where Outline's real architectural sophistication lives. Commands like documentCreator, documentMover, and documentUpdater encapsulate multi-model business logic

Production MCP Server (server/tools/) -- HIGH. ... Filters tool registration by OAuth scopes (AuthenticationHelper.canAccess()) ... Bridges MCP context to existing API infrastructure (buildAPIContext())
Use docling as T28's document extraction backend instead of building one.

themes.name: Media/audio/video extraction pipelines
themes.description: Frames → Shots (camera continuity) → Scenes (LLM narrative segmentation) — each level has summaries, timestamps, metadata.

evidence.quote_or_ref: Multi-granularity hierarchy (shot-analysis.py + scene-analysis.py): Frames -> Shots (camera continuity via similarity threshold) -> Scenes (LLM narrative segmentation)
Smart deduplication (extr-srv-sample-video-dedup.py + extr-srv-sample-video-dedup-faiss.py): Two approaches -- OpenSearch vector similarity (cloud) and FAISS cosine similarity (local)
Per-frame ML feature extraction (extr-srv-image-extraction.py): Each Rekognition API call (labels, text, celebrity, moderation) is independently toggleable
The FAISS version is directly portable. Claims 50% frame reduction = 50% cost reduction

themes.name: CLI ergonomics and build-your-own patterns
themes.description: Python API + CLI + SKILL.md + skill install command + CI monitoring the external API — one package is also one skill.

evidence.quote_or_ref: It's not just a Python wrapper for Google NotebookLM -- it's a complete agent-native package: Python API + CLI + SKILL.md + skill install command + multi-agent support + CI
Module-level variables like `convert_single_video = 1` and `openai_api_key = 'REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_API_KEY'` are the opposite of twelve-factor app configuration
qmd ships its MCP server as a CLI subcommand (qmd mcp). Three transports shown: Stdio (default, per-client subprocess) HTTP foreground HTTP daemon with PID file management
Zero manual schema definitions -- they introspect Click CLI metadata and generate MCP tools dynamically. ~200 lines. Cleanest MCP implementation in the T29 corpus.

themes.name: Documentation-code drift and stale-docs anti-pattern
themes.description: 10.5k-star repo's "How it works" section is a DeepWiki image badge — zero first-party exposition.

evidence.quote_or_ref: the README.md section titled "How it works" is literally an image link to deepwiki.com/jina-ai/reader — a third-party auto-generated wiki. There is zero first-party architecture exposition
1162 files and one mermaid diagram. A new contributor learns the architecture by reading controllers, services, and CLAUDE.md
DOCUMENTATION_PROMISES_CODE_GAPS -- Docs describe features code implements partially. on_state_change callback does not exist.
Outline's README delegates all setup, deployment, and API docs to docs.getoutline.com. For an open-source project wanting contributors, this creates friction

themes.name: Scraping / adversarial-API patterns
themes.description: The most sophisticated code isn't caption fetching — it's proxy rotation (Webshare, GenericProxyConfig, retry-on-429, connection-close for IP rotation).

evidence.quote_or_ref: IP blocking is the real engineering problem. The most sophisticated code in this library isn't the transcript fetching -- it's the proxy infrastructure
Nitter understands adversarial infrastructure -- how to build a service that works against the interests of its data source.
limiting before hitting 429, not after
Twitter killed nitter by changing APIs. The project has 12.8K stars but is effectively dead.

themes.name: AI-readable documentation standards (llms.txt, CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md)
themes.description: AI suggests non-existent packages that attackers register — a risk class specific to AI code generation that traditional security checklists miss.

evidence.quote_or_ref: hallucinated dependencies (slopsquatting) category is particularly valuable — it's a risk class specific to AI code generation that traditional security checklists miss entirely
Composio publishes AI-readable site indexes at `/llms.txt` and `/llms-full.txt`
The CLAUDE.md is quietly one of the best developer onboarding documents in the Python open-source ecosystem. It encodes philosophy, not just setup.

themes.name: AI-generated code risks and anti-patterns
themes.description: 18 structured categories of AI code errors spanning architecture, security, ops, process, compliance — a practitioner lookup table not a thesis.

evidence.quote_or_ref: a dense 6-page reference table that catalogs 18 distinct error categories in AI-generated code. Each category gets a row with description, concrete examples, impact/risks, mitigation strategy
hallucinated dependencies (slopsquatting) category is particularly valuable — it's a risk class specific to AI code generation that traditional security checklists miss entirely
the document identifies "context momentum" (early AI misinterpretations steering projects wrong) and suggests "clear AI context periodically"
No severity ranking. All 18 categories are presented as equal. In practice, hard-coded secrets and injection/XSS cause more damage faster than naming inconsistency

themes.name: Container / deployment security hardening
themes.description: Single Docker image deployed 6× to Cloud Run by varying --args; tsyringe DI resolves the correct API host class at boot.

evidence.quote_or_ref: One Docker image deploys six times to Cloud Run -- crawl, search, serp × us-central1, europe-west1, asia-east2 -- by varying --args
CSP is permissive. connect-src: * in the Content Security Policy is justified by a Safari WebSocket bug that was fixed in Safari 15. This is security debt that hasn't been cleaned up
Most projects treat Docker as a packaging format; nitter treats it as a security boundary.
The XSS vulnerability (unescaped tweet content via Karax verbatim) is a critical flaw

themes.name: Architectural separation (backend/pipeline, command pattern, presenter)
themes.description: ~20 command files encapsulate business logic; both HTTP API and MCP tools delegate to the same commands. Adding a new surface is cheap.

evidence.quote_or_ref: The command pattern (server/commands/) is where Outline's real architectural sophistication lives. Commands like documentCreator, documentMover, and documentUpdater encapsulate multi-model business logic
This three-layer separation (parsing → processing → output) is exactly the kind of clean architecture that survives scaling.
MinerU understands that **document parsing is an orchestration problem, not a model problem**.

themes.name: Solo-maintainer / adoption-hostile governance
themes.description: 33.7K stars + 403 issues + 27 contributors + no CONTRIBUTING/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.

evidence.quote_or_ref: 33.7K stars, 403 open issues, 27 contributors, no CONTRIBUTING.md, no CODE_OF_CONDUCT. Optimized for users, not for building a contributor community
27K stars, 0/29 changesets reviewed. If JASON-OS scales, enforce review from day one.
The README claims CC0 but there's no LICENSE file. GitHub's API reports 'no license.' This means automated tools (including yours) will flag it incorrectly.

themes.name: Meta-tool / dynamic tool discovery over static tool registries
themes.description: Agent uses 6 meta-tools (search/get-schema/execute/…) to discover tools at runtime, not load all 1000+.

evidence.quote_or_ref: Composio provides 6 meta tools — search, get-schema, auth, execute, workbench, bash. The agent discovers tools at runtime and loads only what's needed

No mention of tools that audit your tools — skill audits, hook ecosystem audits, pattern compliance checking, learning effectiveness analysis. The article treats Claude Code as a product to configure, not a platform to build on.

themes.name: Convention over configuration (filename, grep-friendly, unique-names)
themes.description: 498-line CLAUDE.md encodes philosophy (grep-friendly naming, minimize-unique-names, NO MOCKS) beyond pure setup instructions.

evidence.quote_or_ref: The CLAUDE.md is quietly one of the best developer onboarding documents in the Python open-source ecosystem. It encodes philosophy, not just setup.
Most ML projects treat their output schema as an implementation detail. MinerU treats it as a product surface.

themes.name: Schema-first validation (Zod, type safety, runtime contracts)
themes.description: Tools only visible if token has matching scope; Zod validation, tracing wrapper, presenters. Most mature MCP reference analyzed.

evidence.quote_or_ref: Production MCP Server (server/tools/) -- HIGH. ... Filters tool registration by OAuth scopes (AuthenticationHelper.canAccess()) ... Bridges MCP context to existing API infrastructure (buildAPIContext())

themes.name: Error handling, sanitization, and safety patterns
themes.description: Shell injection + path traversal + credential exposure in a PDF processing tool.

evidence.quote_or_ref: Shell injection in chunk_convert.py (shell=True with user input), path traversal in file upload, AWS credentials as CLI defaults

ecosystem_gaps.description: SoNash is Sober Nashville — a recovery notebook for the sobriety community. The 32-source corpus contains zero references to recovery-specific UX, peer-support workflows, trauma-informed design, or harm-reduction content patterns.
ecosystem_gaps.why_unfilled: User has been building SoNash's infrastructure layer via JASON-OS research; product-layer sources were not prioritized in early analysis waves.
ecosystem_gaps.suggested_action: Queue /website-analysis on Sober Grid, InTheRooms, I Am Sober, and 12-step digital tool references. Add /document-analysis on 42 CFR Part 2 (SUD confidentiality) policy docs.

ecosystem_gaps.domain: Firebase-native architecture (App Check, Firestore rules, Cloud Functions)
ecosystem_gaps.description: SoNash's stack is Firebase 12.10 (Firestore + Auth + App Check + Functions). Zero sources in the corpus are Firebase-native.
ecosystem_gaps.why_unfilled: Corpus skews to Python/Rust/Go infrastructure repos and Node-agnostic tool ecosystems.
ecosystem_gaps.suggested_action: Add one high-quality Firebase production reference (e.g., Next.js+Firebase SaaS starter with App Check enforced, or Firebase Extensions gallery) to Wave 6.

ecosystem_gaps.description: SoNash vision is Privacy-First. Corpus extraction sources all send content to third parties (r.jina.ai, Google Innertube, AWS Rekognition, Webshare proxies).
ecosystem_gaps.why_unfilled: Extraction-as-SaaS is the dominant ecosystem model; privacy-first is a minority posture.
ecosystem_gaps.suggested_action: Queue whisper.cpp, monolith, SingleFile, readable-cli for Wave 6 local-first extraction coverage.

ecosystem_gaps.description: Sobriety data is regulated under 42 CFR Part 2 (substance-use disorder confidentiality). Bedrock's tokenize/untokenize pattern is the closest corpus reference but covers only the LLM boundary.
ecosystem_gaps.why_unfilled: Regulatory content is not typically analyzed through /analyze skills; requires targeted document sourcing.
ecosystem_gaps.suggested_action: Add a policy-document analysis of 42 CFR Part 2 or a privacy-first journal app case study (e.g., Signal protocol implementation walkthrough).

ecosystem_gaps.description: Corpus MCP patterns are all Python (archivebox, hkuds, graphify) or Ruby/ES (outline). JASON-OS targets Node/TS.
ecosystem_gaps.why_unfilled: MCP adoption in 2026 is Python-heavy; TS reference implementations are newer.
ecosystem_gaps.suggested_action: Analyze @modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk reference servers or TS-based FastMCP equivalents.

ecosystem_gaps.description: errors-and-vulnerabilities document flags slopsquatting as a real AI-native supply-chain risk. No corpus source shows a production detection implementation.
ecosystem_gaps.why_unfilled: Pre-install package verification tooling is nascent (2025+).
ecosystem_gaps.suggested_action: Analyze Dep-Hallucinator or equivalent; draft a pre-commit/pre-install gate.

ecosystem_gaps.description: No corpus source covers SBOM generation or license scanning.
ecosystem_gaps.why_unfilled: Compliance tooling is outside the current corpus focus.
ecosystem_gaps.suggested_action: Pick one tool (cyclonedx, license-checker) for targeted analysis.

ecosystem_gaps.description: codecrafters + hkuds expose the failure mode (entries grow without retirement). No corpus source demonstrates a working deprecation process.
ecosystem_gaps.why_unfilled: Retirement patterns are typically undocumented; only visible in changelog archaeology.
ecosystem_gaps.suggested_action: Analyze projects with mature deprecation (Rails deprecation_warning, PEP 387, npm deprecate CLI).

ecosystem_gaps.description: SoNash has 14 pre-commit checks + TDMS tech-debt pipeline. firecrawl's CLAUDE.md capability-gating is close but narrower. No corpus source covers a full governance pipeline comparable to SoNash's.
ecosystem_gaps.why_unfilled: Governance-pipeline repos are fewer than application repos in 2026 ecosystem.
ecosystem_gaps.suggested_action: Analyze pre-commit/pre-commit, lefthook, or husky ecosystem patterns.

ecosystem_gaps.description: crawl4ai has resume_state but it's in-memory-only. T28 Gap Agent A Q5 asks for progressive extraction with durable state.
ecosystem_gaps.why_unfilled: Most extraction pipelines run to completion in-process; durable execution is a separate ecosystem.
ecosystem_gaps.suggested_action: Analyze Temporal.io workflow engine, durable-execution pattern, or EventStore architecture references.

ecosystem_gaps.description: Bedrock repo flags local-first as the right direction; corpus has no Whisper-native reference.
ecosystem_gaps.why_unfilled: Local-first ML is a minority posture.
ecosystem_gaps.suggested_action: Analyze whisper.cpp or mlx-whisper as a direct reference.

candidates.name: scrapeURL engine-fallback chain
candidates.description: Pluggable extraction engines coordinated by a fallback-list builder. buildFallbackList → scrapeURLWithEngine → try next or NoEnginesLeftError. Named failure mode, documented order, engine-level telemetry.

candidates.name: Frame sampling + smart dedup pipeline
candidates.description: moviepy frame extraction with configurable FPS + FAISS/OpenSearch similarity dedup. Portable pattern for local video processing.

candidates.name: Toggleable per-frame ML feature extraction
candidates.description: Each ML feature independently enabled. Config-driven feature selection. Extensibility pattern for adding new capabilities.

candidates.name: Evaluation Service prompt template system
candidates.description: Templates for moderation, summarization, IAB classification. Run arbitrary LLM prompts against extracted metadata.

candidates.name: Tiered fetch fallback chain with sideLoad body hint
candidates.description: Firestore cache -> curl -> proxy retry -> Puppeteer with sideLoad hint -> Readability -> Turndown -> stale cache fallback. Reference for /website-analysis pipeline evolution beyond superpowers-chrome.

candidates.name: Readability + Turndown + Puppeteer extraction pipeline
candidates.description: Industry-standard HTML->markdown stack. All stages swappable via DI. If /website-analysis needs non-Chrome fallback, HTML + @mozilla/readability covers happy path with small dep footprint.

candidates.name: Over-engineered install mechanism
candidates.description: 280 lines for 'copy a file and stamp a version.' Extract the pattern, not the complexity.

candidates.name: youtube-transcript-api as T27 primary extraction layer
candidates.description: pip install youtube-transcript-api. One-line caption fetch with language selection, translation, SRT/WebVTT output. 7K stars, MIT, 100% coverage, 2 deps. First adoption recommendation in analysis series.

candidates.name: 3-tier resource pool (PERMANENT/HOT/COLD)
candidates.description: Browser pool with tiered warmth and janitor lifecycle. 10x memory claim. Resource pooling for JASON-OS agent pools / MCP connections.

candidates.name: x-* request header protocol
candidates.description: Runtime config surface via headers (11+ headers). Cleaner than query-string pollution. Applicable when CAS exposes /website-analysis over MCP or as agent-facing tool surface.

candidates.name: Embedded Task() Subagent Patterns
candidates.description: SKILL.md includes literal Task() invocations for agent copy-paste. Reduces cognitive load for invoking subagents.

candidates.name: AGENTS.md separation pattern
candidates.description: Separate file for Codex/parallel agent guidance instead of embedding in CLAUDE.md. Consider if CLAUDE.md grows further.

candidates.name: CLAUDE.md Agent Guidance Pattern
candidates.description: CLAUDE.md structure comparison + ambient PR workflow.

candidates.name: Blocking poll anti-pattern
candidates.description: Transcribe handler polls with time.sleep(30) in a blocking while loop. Acceptable for CLI, but must not be copied into agent pipelines. Use async callbacks or event-driven patterns instead.

candidates.name: Backend + Pipeline separation pattern
candidates.description: Backends handle format-specific parsing, pipelines orchestrate processing stages. T28 should separate source extraction from analysis orchestration.

candidates.name: program.md Agent Instruction Pattern
candidates.description: 114-line autonomous research protocol: setup, experiment loop, keep/discard, crash recovery, NEVER STOP, simplicity criterion, TSV logging. Compare against SKILL.md.

candidates.name: Multi-granularity hierarchy (frame → shot → scene)
candidates.description: Three-level temporal decomposition. Each level has summaries, timestamps, metadata. Structures how video content is analyzed.

candidates.name: Subtitle-to-frame timestamp alignment
candidates.description: Map Transcribe subtitle segments to frame timestamps. Non-obvious engineering for multimodal analysis.

candidates.name: OpenSearch dependency for similarity (anti-pattern)
candidates.description: FAISS alternative exists in same repo. Don't add search cluster for video dedup.

candidates.name: Multi-provider fallback generator (iterProviders)
candidates.description: Generator yields provider clients in preference order; for/of catches per-provider errors and advances. Small port (~40 lines). Reuse across deep-research, multi-SERP, transcript providers, embedding providers.

candidates.name: Innertube API reverse-engineering approach
candidates.description: YouTube captions fetched via undocumented /youtubei/v1/player API with ANDROID client context. No API key needed. Fragile but library maintains compatibility across 8 years.

candidates.name: Validation without maintenance trap
candidates.description: Format validation scripts ensure structural integrity of new entries, but don't ensure entries are current, links are live, or categories are relevant. Missing piece: lifecycle management.

candidates.name: No tests (anti-pattern)
candidates.description: 52 Python files, 1 test. AWS reference architectures often skip tests. Don't follow this pattern.

candidates.name: Commercial OSS without tests as an architectural stance
candidates.description: Reader runs at scale with zero tests. Quality floor = TypeScript + lint + observability + traffic volume. Defensible for originator, does not transfer to adopters.

candidates.name: Zero tests in a 10k-star production service
candidates.description: Reader has no test suite. Quality = TypeScript strict + lint + traffic. Works for originator (observability), fails for adopters. 'No tests' requires compensating monitoring, not the other way around.

candidates.name: TestServer ephemeral-port integration testing
candidates.description: Custom test utility that starts a real Koa HTTP server on port 0 for integration tests. Tests exercise middleware, routing, and serialization through the actual transport.

candidates.name: IP ban workaround operational guide
candidates.description: Production-tested guide to handling YouTube IP blocks: generic proxies, Webshare rotating residential IPs, retry strategies.

candidates.name: 100% coverage + 2-dep library packaging
candidates.description: Poetry with lock file, py.typed marker, --fail-under=100, only requests + defusedxml runtime deps. Reference for well-packaged Python library.

candidates.name: Autonomy Rules Section in SKILL.md
candidates.description: 22 auto-run + 7 ask-first with per-command reasons. Structured encoding of autonomy guardrails. Retrofit candidate for add-debt, pr-review, session-end, audit-* skills.

candidates.name: Claude Code plugin manifest (marketplace.json)
candidates.description: Plugin manifest bundling MCP server + skills + metadata for 'claude plugin marketplace add' installation. Single source of truth for Claude Code plugin discovery.

candidates.name: PluginManager hook type registry
candidates.description: 10 hook types (API, AuthProvider, EmailTemplate, Processor, Task, SearchProvider, etc.). Priority-based ordering. Auto-discovery via glob. plugin.json manifests. Structural client/server/shared split per plugin.

candidates.name: Skill Install + Version Stamping
candidates.description: skill.py reads SKILL.md from package data, stamps version, writes to ~/.claude/skills/. Directly retires cross_locale_config problem. Needs Node port.

candidates.name: Ambient CLAUDE.md PR Workflow
candidates.description: 4-step embedded PR workflow (monitor CI, check reviews, fix + reply with SHA, verify mergeStateStatus CLEAN). Alternative to invoked /pr-review.

candidates.name: Hook lifecycle with 8 ordered hooks
candidates.description: 8 lifecycle hooks with clear sequence and security model. Combine with ArchiveBox governance for JASON-OS hook ecosystem.

candidates.name: Open Skills Ecosystem Investigation
candidates.description: npx skills add teng-lin/notebooklm-py. Unverified. Could be a real skill registry relevant to JASON-OS distribution.

candidates.name: YouTube audio extraction pipeline
candidates.description: pytubefix download + moviepy audio extraction to MP3. Full lifecycle in ~30 lines. Reference for T27 YouTube content extraction.

candidates.name: Amazon Transcribe async job lifecycle
candidates.description: Start transcription job -> poll -> fetch transcript from S3 -> cleanup. Shows speaker labels, language config, S3 output routing.

candidates.name: Don't build your own transcript fetcher (anti-pattern)
candidates.description: 8 years and 399 commits maintaining compatibility with YouTube's changing API. Use the library, don't replicate the maintenance burden.

candidates.name: Graceful dependency checking
candidates.description: dependency_exists() checks at runtime, logs warning, falls back. T28 needs this for optional deps (Whisper, Chrome, gh CLI).

candidates.name: Monolithic single-file tool anti-pattern
candidates.description: 280 lines, no CLI, no config file, hard-coded vars. Works for personal use, impossible to integrate.

candidates.name: Hard-coded source config anti-pattern
candidates.description: Module-level variables for config including API keys. No CLI args, no env vars. Anti-twelve-factor.

candidates.name: MCP server as CLI subcommand (3 transports)
candidates.description: MCP server accessed via 'qmd mcp' subcommand. Stdio + HTTP foreground + HTTP daemon with PID file. Daemon avoids expensive model reload.

candidates.name: Fixture-based eval harness with difficulty tiers
candidates.description: Test queries categorized easy/medium/hard with expected docs. Precision@k, recall, MRR, F1 across 4 backends. 'qmd bench' CLI for reproducible quality measurement.

candidates.name: MCP auto-discovery from CLI metadata
candidates.description: Zero-schema MCP server that introspects Click CLI commands and auto-generates MCP tool definitions. ~200 lines. Uses CliRunner for execution. Eliminates manual schema duplication.

candidates.name: MCP OAuth scope-filtered tool registration
candidates.description: Production MCP server that filters tool registration based on OAuth scopes. Tools only visible if token has matching scope. Uses @modelcontextprotocol/sdk with Zod validation.

candidates.name: Command pattern (transport-agnostic business logic)
candidates.description: Business logic in transport-agnostic command files (~20 commands). Both HTTP API routes and MCP tools delegate to same commands. Enables adding new surfaces without duplicating logic.

candidates.name: buildAPIContext() MCP-to-API bridge
candidates.description: Bridge function that constructs a Koa-style APIContext from MCP request context. Enables MCP tools to reuse existing API middleware and command infrastructure without duplication.

candidates.name: Presenter response formatting layer
candidates.description: JSON response formatting layer between models and API/MCP consumers. Each model has a presenter controlling exposed fields and formatting.

candidates.name: docling-mcp as extraction backend
candidates.description: MCP server wrapping docling conversion. T28 could use as document extraction backend instead of building one.

candidates.name: Colab notebook: full-site crawling methodology
candidates.description: The only practical multi-URL example using r.jina.ai. Useful IF /website-analysis adds a bulk mode later.

candidates.name: Results TSV Logging Convention
candidates.description: commit/val_bpb/memory_gb/status/description. Already have JSONL equivalent.

candidates.name: Single-file-everything pattern
candidates.description: 390 links in one README.md. Works at small scale, unmaintainable at large scale. Watch for same impulse in EXTRACTIONS.md, MEMORY.md, SKILL_INDEX.md.

candidates.name: Async task state machine with TTL cleanup
candidates.description: In-memory task dict with four states (pending/processing/completed/failed), asyncio.Queue work channel, asyncio.Semaphore concurrency cap, background sweep for TTL cleanup, FS-backed output artifacts keyed by task ID. Ephemeral state. Answers T28 Gap Agent A Q5 (progressive extraction).

candidates.name: Sliding window for long-input memory bounding
candidates.description: get_processing_window_size(default=64) applied identically across all three backends. Pages loaded, inferred, freed in window-sized batches. Peak memory strictly bounded.

candidates.name: Graph-only persistence without versioning
candidates.description: graph.json has no schema_version, no migration path. Format changes break old graphs silently. SoNash analysis.json schema_version is the right approach.

candidates.name: curl-impersonate + TLS fingerprint concept
candidates.description: Understanding that anti-bot defenses fingerprint the full TLS/TCP stack explains why firecrawl/crawl4ai leak through some sites.

candidates.name: curl-impersonate as Docker LD_PRELOAD base layer
candidates.description: Two-stage Docker build: FROM lwthiker/curl-impersonate -> FROM node:22. Copy .so + LD_PRELOAD. Template for any future self-hosted fetcher that needs to defeat anti-bot TLS fingerprinting.

candidates.name: Proxy rotation infrastructure (Webshare)
candidates.description: ProxyConfig ABC -> GenericProxyConfig, WebshareProxyConfig. Rotating residential IPs, retry-on-429, connection-close for forced rotation.

candidates.name: Session pool with per-endpoint rate limiting
candidates.description: Multiple sessions rotate on rate limits. Per-session per-endpoint tracking with preemptive backoff.

candidates.name: ADVERSARIAL_DEPENDENCY
candidates.description: Building product on scraping hostile platform. Twitter killed nitter by changing APIs. 12.8K stars, effectively dead.

candidates.name: Multi-agent orchestration via openai_swarm
candidates.description: Reference implementation of multi-agent coordination around a single scraping task. Worth reading to inform SoNash agent teams and JASON-OS agent boundary design.

candidates.name: Deep Research API usage pattern
candidates.description: How firecrawl's Deep Research API is invoked in practice (apartment-finder). Interactive preference gathering → research call → LLM analysis → structured JSON.

candidates.name: Whisper tuned params (beam_size=10, vad_filter)
candidates.description: beam_size=10 (vs default 5) for accuracy. vad_filter=True to skip silence and prevent Whisper hallucination-on-silence issue.

candidates.name: 58-example cookbook pattern
candidates.description: The examples/ directory as product-marketing-in-code. 58 self-contained apps, each pairing firecrawl with a specific LLM or framework.

candidates.name: init()-based plugin registry with domain routing
candidates.description: Single-method Extractor interface + global map + init() auto-registration + blank imports + universal fallback. 46 extractors, zero boilerplate. Go-specific but principle universal.

candidates.name: Hallucinated dependencies (slopsquatting)
candidates.description: AI suggests non-existent packages. Verify against registries, use SBOM, use Dep-Hallucinator tool.

candidates.name: Context momentum / hallucinated logic
candidates.description: Early AI misinterpretations steer wrong. Clear context periodically, use separate threads per phase.

candidates.name: Structural entropy (code bloat)
candidates.description: AI generates verbose, redundant code. Act as ruthless editor. Use git diffs, static analysis.

candidates.name: 15-entry checkpoint cycle pattern
candidates.description: Periodic maintenance rhythm during long operations. Every N items: rebuild indexes, audit quality, check for anti-patterns. Mid-session companion to session-end.

candidates.name: No-license-on-purpose trap
candidates.description: Karpathy can get away with no license because he's Karpathy. 9,611 forks in legal ambiguity. Don't follow this example for JASON-OS.

candidates.name: GPL_LICENSE_TRAP
candidates.description: GPL-3.0 copyleft blocks adoption as dependency. Must extract patterns only, never import code.

candidates.name: CONTRIBUTOR_HOSTILE_GOVERNANCE
candidates.description: 33.7K stars, 403 issues, no CONTRIBUTING, no CODE_OF_CONDUCT. Users optimized, contributors not.

candidates.name: SOLO_MAINTAINER_GOVERNANCE
candidates.description: 27K stars but 0/29 changesets with code review. Architectural decisions in prose docs, not enforced contracts. 100% community health score masks governance gap.

candidates.name: PERMISSIVE_DEFAULTS_FOR_SELF_HOSTED
candidates.description: ALLOWED_HOSTS='*', SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=False, no HTTPS enforcement. Security-by-configuration rather than security-by-default.

candidates.name: Strategy ABC with 55+ implementations
candidates.description: Clean abstract hierarchy with runtime composition. Skill composition reference.

candidates.name: Abstract generic Store<T> with request deduplication
candidates.description: MobX base Store with generic typing, observable data Map, standardized RPC actions, and request dedup via Symbol-keyed promise tracking. Prevents N+1 fetch patterns.

candidates.name: DOCUMENTATION_PROMISES_CODE_GAPS
candidates.description: Docs ahead of code reality. on_state_change does not exist. Verify code matches docs before recommending.

candidates.name: DeepWiki-as-architecture-docs
candidates.description: README 'How it works' is a DeepWiki image badge. No first-party architecture exposition. Directly anti-example for SoNash docs philosophy.

candidates.name: Zero architecture prose docs
candidates.description: 1162 files, one mermaid diagram, no docs/ directory. New contributors pay the ramp-up tax. File as evidence in 'do we need this ADR?' conversations.

candidates.name: DOCUMENTATION_ABSENT_FOR_CONTRIBUTORS
candidates.description: 46 extractors, 30 contributors, 23-line CONTRIBUTING.md, zero architecture docs. Growth without onboarding = bus-factor risk.

candidates.name: Hook execution model with ordering and bg/fg lifecycle
candidates.description: Production-grade hook system: event-family naming convention, 2-digit ordering prefix, foreground/background distinction, SIGTERM finalization for background hooks, JSONL stdout contract.

candidates.name: CLAUDE.md as structured developer philosophy document
candidates.description: 498-line CLAUDE.md encoding not just setup instructions but development philosophy: grep-friendly naming, minimize-unique-names, NO MOCKS testing standard, coverage-as-dead-code methodology.

candidates.name: Strict assertion testing convention
candidates.description: Use exact counts (==) not loose bounds (>=) in test assertions. Forces tests to catch regressions rather than just pass.

candidates.name: Structured output contract as first-class document
candidates.description: Human-readable specification of middle.json IR: typed fields, 4-level block hierarchy, enumerated block types, bbox convention, explicit breaking-change callouts between pipeline and VLM backends.

candidates.name: CSP wildcard security debt
candidates.description: connect-src: * justified by Safari WebSocket bug fixed in Safari 15. Temporary workaround that became permanent debt.

candidates.name: Docker container hardening (RO fs + CAP_DROP ALL)
candidates.description: Read-only filesystem, CAP_DROP ALL, non-root uid 998, alpine minimal, health checks. Best Docker hardening in T29 corpus.

candidates.name: SECURITY_HEADERS_ABSENT
candidates.description: No CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS in web application. Zero defense-in-depth.

candidates.name: 18-category AI error taxonomy
candidates.description: Structured reference table of AI code generation failure modes with examples, impact, and mitigations. Cross-reference against CODE_PATTERNS.md.

candidates.name: llms.txt standard for AI-readable documentation
candidates.description: Standardized llms.txt and llms-full.txt at site root for LLM consumption. Makes docs AI-discoverable.

candidates.name: Safe expression eval via AST
candidates.description: User expressions validated: blocks imports, dunders, dangerous builtins. Security for user expressions.

candidates.name: SECURITY_AS_AFTERTHOUGHT
candidates.description: Shell injection, path traversal, credential exposure in a tool processing untrusted PDFs.

candidates.name: Silent retry workflow converts flake into green
candidates.description: Automatic retry of failed CI runs up to 3x without surfacing the flake rate. Opposite of silent-failure-hunter discipline.

candidates.name: Meta-tool pattern for dynamic tool discovery
candidates.description: Instead of loading all tool definitions, provide a small set of meta tools (search, get-schema, execute). Agent discovers at runtime. Solves context window bloat. Applicable to JASON-OS at 100+ skills.

covered.domain: Claude Code as a platform (skills, plugins, commands, CLAUDE.md)
MCP as a first-class integration surface
Meta-tool / dynamic tool discovery over static tool registries
Schema-first validation (Zod, type safety, runtime contracts)
Error handling, sanitization, and safety patterns

gaps.suggested_scan: Queue /website-analysis on Sober Grid, InTheRooms, I Am Sober, and 12-step digital tool references. Add /document-analysis on 42 CFR Part 2 (SUD confidentiality) policy docs.

gaps.domain: Firebase-native architecture (App Check, Firestore rules, Cloud Functions)
gaps.suggested_scan: Add one high-quality Firebase production reference (e.g., Next.js+Firebase SaaS starter with App Check enforced, or Firebase Extensions gallery) to Wave 6.
Queue whisper.cpp, monolith, SingleFile, readable-cli for Wave 6 local-first extraction coverage.
Add a policy-document analysis of 42 CFR Part 2 or a privacy-first journal app case study (e.g., Signal protocol implementation walkthrough).
Analyze @modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk reference servers or TS-based FastMCP equivalents.
Analyze Dep-Hallucinator or equivalent; draft a pre-commit/pre-install gate.
Pick one tool (cyclonedx, license-checker) for targeted analysis.
Analyze projects with mature deprecation (Rails deprecation_warning, PEP 387, npm deprecate CLI).
Analyze pre-commit/pre-commit, lefthook, or husky ecosystem patterns.
Analyze Temporal.io workflow engine, durable-execution pattern, or EventStore architecture references.
Analyze whisper.cpp or mlx-whisper as a direct reference.

opportunity_matrix.title: Publish SoNash /llms.txt from CLAUDE.md + SKILL.md corpus
opportunity_matrix.description: One-shot script that generates llms.txt from existing docs. Makes SoNash discoverable by other LLM systems without custom integration.

opportunity_matrix.title: Add slopsquatting gate to pre-commit (package-name verification)
opportunity_matrix.description: Pre-install check: verify npm package names against registry + dep-hallucinator-style heuristics. Closes an AI-native supply-chain risk SoNash currently does not gate.

opportunity_matrix.title: Adopt .claude-plugin/marketplace.json as SoNash skill distribution format
opportunity_matrix.description: If SoNash ever distributes skills outside the single repo, the emerging marketplace.json format is the standard. Cheap to preemptively adopt.

opportunity_matrix.title: Queue 3-5 recovery-community UX sources for Wave 6 /analyze
opportunity_matrix.description: Biggest gap in corpus. Sober Grid, I Am Sober, InTheRooms, peer-support UX. Fills the single most critical absence (3-agent confirmation).

opportunity_matrix.title: Add bidirectional doc-feature validator to SoNash pre-commit
opportunity_matrix.description: Inward check (cross-doc-deps) already exists. Outward check (code feature has doc reference) closes the drift loop.

opportunity_matrix.title: Prototype zero-schema MCP server from SoNash scripts/ CLI introspection
opportunity_matrix.description: SoNash has 100+ scripts/ with argparse/yargs. An MCP server introspecting them eliminates the schema-drift class entirely. JASON-OS Domain 02a candidate.

opportunity_matrix.title: Research OAuth-scope-filtered MCP tool registration for JASON-OS
opportunity_matrix.description: Outline's production pattern. Before JASON-OS adds any MCP tool, this is the security model worth studying in depth.

opportunity_matrix.title: Prototype meta-tool pattern for SoNash skill/agent discovery
opportunity_matrix.description: 72+ skills + 38 agents. Loading all at session start is expensive. A meta-skill that searches and loads on demand follows composio's playbook.

opportunity_matrix.title: Design SoNash Firebase-native reference doc
opportunity_matrix.description: Architectural write-up + diagrams. App Check posture, Firestore rules, httpsCallable gateway, emulator patterns. Becomes SoNash's reference for onboarding AI agents to the stack.

opportunity_matrix.title: Explore privacy-first on-device extraction for SoNash content analysis
opportunity_matrix.description: whisper.cpp for local ASR, monolith/SingleFile for local HTML, readable-cli for local Reader-mode. If SoNash ever analyzes user-provided content, privacy-first local-first is the vision statement commitment.

opportunity_matrix.title: Design 42 CFR Part 2 / HIPAA-adjacent data-handling architecture for SoNash
opportunity_matrix.description: Compliance question, not coding. Should be scoped before SoNash adds any storage pattern that touches sobriety-specific data.

opportunity_matrix.title: Spec a skill retirement workflow for SoNash .claude/skills/
opportunity_matrix.description: As SoNash accumulates skills (72+ today), retirement has to be a first-class motion. Draft a deprecation process: warning phase, deprecation_warning-style surface, removal.

reading_chain.rationale: Rhetorical framing: ingest/query/lint vocabulary for CAS-like systems.
AI-code risk taxonomy. Sets the 'what could go wrong' frame.
Minimalist orchestration thesis. Read before kieranklaassen-gist for counterpoint.
Swarm 6-pattern orchestration taxonomy. Counter-thesis to karpathy-autoresearch.
Comprehensive Claude Code as platform overview.
Curated catalog of build-your-own projects. Meta-source for discovery.
Meta-tool architecture + native vs MCP tradeoff + llms.txt standard. Essential for JASON-OS Domain 02a thinking.
Personal knowledge wiki skill building tutorial. Short-form.
Exploration-over-conclusion design principle. Low-signal but fast read.
Skill-building video overview. Self-referential confirmation of SoNash practices.
Obsidian attachment tutorial. Low direct relevance; read only if considering file-based retrieval systems.
Catalog of 1000+ public APIs. Useful as a feature-hook reference for SoNash extensions.
Extraction pipeline reference architecture. Start here for extraction topic.
Type-detection + auto-partitioning. Pairs with docling.
Engine-fallback scraping. Pairs with crawl4ai.
Strategy hierarchy + filter/scorer composition.
3-stage pipeline + benchmark registry. PDF-specific.
Async task state machine + VLM integration.
'LLM-friendly URL extraction' as product. Anti-pattern examples included.
Multi-granularity media pipeline (frame->shot->scene). Novel architecture.
PII tokenize/untokenize pattern (high SoNash relevance).
Caption-first + Whisper fallback.
Per-site plugin pattern.
Adopt-as-library (rare pattern).
Production MCP server + command pattern + plugin system. Primary MCP implementation reference.
Zero-schema MCP + hooks model + CLAUDE.md philosophy.
CLI as MCP transport.
MCP as CLI subcommand + local-first inference.
Stateless token-budgeted MCP server + knowledge graph.
Flat memory skill approach.
Notebooklm-style doc chat.
Docker hardening + ADVERSARIAL_DEPENDENCY anti-pattern. Read last for the cautionary framing.

interest_shifts.from: tooling research (what a system should look like)
interest_shifts.to: tooling consolidation (how specific production systems actually work)
Claude Code as platform with skills, plugins, hooks, marketplaces
